expectations.evidence: Forbidden substring absent; response uses '<the params name you populated>' as a placeholder and warns against hardcoding.

expectations.text: Leads with a discovery step (merge_restrict or fetch as_dict) before narrowing
expectations.evidence: Step 1 (titled 'discover what those 14 rows actually are') uses `PositionOutput.merge_restrict(key)` and `(PositionOutput & key).fetch(as_dict=True)` before any narrowing.

expectations.text: Does not assume Trodes or DLC without evidence from the prompt
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly says 'Don't assume Trodes' and conditions Trodes-specific advice on 'if Step 1 shows your row came from TrodesPosV1'.

expectations.text: Names the cardinality problem explicitly (too many rows matching)
expectations.evidence: Calls it 'a cardinality (too-loose-restriction) bug' and explains 'your restriction matches 14 rows, fetch1 needs 1'.
